key_insight: In nanocrystalline Al-Ni, amorphous grain-boundary complexions favor homogeneous plasticity via shear-transformation zones, while short-range-ordered complexions increase strength but trigger stress heterogeneity and shear localization.
engineering_relevance: Complexion engineering offers a route to tune the strength–ductility balance in nanocrystalline alloys by controlling grain-boundary structure and temperature history.
method: Large-scale Monte Carlo simulations to generate grain-boundary complexion states
Molecular dynamics simulations of nanocrystalline Al-Ni at 2 at.% and 4 at.% Ni
Comparison of annealing at 913 K versus 378 K to isolate amorphous versus semi-amorphous/SRO behaviors
evidence_signal: The abstract reports two simulation-based complexion states with distinct mechanical responses across two Ni concentrations, linking microstructure directly to plasticity mode and localization behavior.
watch_item: The results are simulation-derived and may depend on alloy composition, annealing condition, and interatomic modeling, so experimental transferability should be validated case by case.
paper_title: Amorphous vs. Short-Range-Ordered Complexions: Consequences for Grain-Boundary-Mediated Plasticity in Nanocrystalline Al-Ni Alloys
